hypothesis: If a multimodal contrastive model is trained to align canonical SMILES with 3D electron density grids, then the learned 3D embeddings will be invariant to molecular rotations and yield superior cross-modal retrieval, pose-diverse retrieval, and improved clustering and linear-probe property prediction compared to unimodal or non-multimodal baselines, because the canonical, pose-independent SMILES modality provides supervisory signal that anchors and regularizes the 3D representation.
